name: rf/series_parallel_rlc
description: Coax in-line RLC resonator: FDTD |S11| notch (series) / peak (parallel) vs the analytic f0 = 1/(2pi sqrt(LC)), tracking C over +/-20%.
target: f_res tracks 1/sqrt(C) (f_res*sqrt(C) const) and moves under +/-20% C
tolerance_basis: Wave-level: the series |S11| notch obeys f_res*sqrt(C)=const (the 1/sqrt(LC) law) to ~1% and moves by the analytic 1/sqrt(C) ratio under +/-20% C; the parallel |S11| peak moves in the correct direction (monotone in C). The absolute f_res sits ~13% below the ideal f0 -- the documented, consistent parasitic (rod-gap fringe capacitance) shift, measured not hidden.
falsification: EXECUTED: a valid RLC bench must track C. Measured -- series f_res*sqrt(C) spread 0.010 (const => tracks 1/sqrt(LC)), -20%C ratio 1.099 (analytic 1.118), +20%C ratio 0.919 (analytic 0.913); parallel peak monotone in C (True). The retired bench's peak did NOT move with C.
notes: REBUILT (coax in-line RLC). The RLC is a two-terminal element in the coax inner conductor ahead of a matched through-PML continuation, so it carries the full axial current and its resonance controls the feed reflection. Absolute f_res ~0.862 x ideal (documented parasitic downshift from the rod-gap fringe capacitance).
